# Drill-down brief - within-country equity: IND

*Stunting (% of children under 5) by household wealth quintile, 2021. Generated by CEDAR - every figure cited below.*

## Key findings

- [!] Children in the **poorest fifth are stunted at 46.1%** versus **22.9% in the richest** - a **2.0x gap** (23.2 percentage points). [1]
- [i] Stunting falls step-by-step across wealth groups (46.1% -> 39.7% -> 34.4% -> 28.1% -> 22.9%) - a socioeconomic gradient to target. [1]
- [caveat] Survey snapshot (2021 DHS/MICS), not an annual series; disaggregated estimates carry wider uncertainty than national totals.

## Methods & reproducibility

Drill-down = the same evidence chain at finer grain: discover wealth-disaggregated indicators -> retrieve each quintile with its own provenance -> verify coverage -> analyse the gap (ratio = poorest/richest; gap = difference in points). No figure is model-generated.
